label: The Shearline Arsenal
description: The Shearline Arsenal occupies a narrow basalt-glass storefront cut directly into Seamline Concord’s faultside terrace, its facade scored with calibrated grooves that catch the copper-white twilight in strict bands. Interior walls are faced with mirror-slag panels that multiply silhouettes and align displays to the city’s auroral sightlines, preserving navigational geometry at the scale of a room. Racks and plinths present edge tools and polearms in vitrified clamps insulated against Lacrymara’s electrostatic glass dust, while a shuttered testing lane runs the length of the shop in grit-darkened stone. A recessed counter of fused basalt contains lockplates and sealed drawers, its surface perpetually filmed with pinned reflective particles from the cistern-plazas.
